read.why_it_failed: The route-owned scalar reducer improves the modeled number of final dKV writes, but the realized kernel stays around 115 ms for token_block 4-32 and gets worse for 64-128. The owner has become serialized around route segments and does not preserve the fast query-major MFMA producer.
read.why_the_dkv_slice_does_not_materialize_e: A late dKV reducer is called after TorchTitan has already paid for selected state, probability or scaled-dP materialization, bridge tensors, and a separate producer. Its local win can be erased by those upstream costs. A real speedup requires moving the reduction into the sparse-attention backward producer itself.
read.required_fused_owner_contract: q,dO,compressed/shared KV,indices,lse/sink -> recompute qk/probs/scaled-dP -> produce dQ -> group-aware dKV epilogue before global memory
read.promotion_gate: Only a full sparse-attention backward S4096 timing that consumes source tensors directly and avoids HSK/prob/scaled-dP slabs should be promoted toward e2e TFLOPs.

preserve: MFMA-shaped qk, dprob, dQ, and dKV contribution math
